data.description:
## Why
Verdict-flip on identical code is a RECALL problem, not a false-positive one: in flipping same-material
review pairs the decisive would-block findings had validity ~0.99 and 14/17 flips were because the
other run simply MISSED the valid finding. Tightening thresholds cannot help; improving finder recall can.

## What
Feed this ticket's prior-review findings (from the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar corpus, matched by `norm_id`)
into Pass-1 as "previously-surfaced concerns - independently re-verify against the CURRENT plan; some
may be resolved." Recall rises because the finder no longer forgets a known defect run-to-run. The
existing Pass-2 verifier is the UNCHANGED false-positive backstop: a concern the current plan fixed
fails validity and is dropped, so a stale primed finding cannot produce a block.

## Scope
- Pass-1 context assembly only; Pass-2/Pass-3 unchanged. Cost is context tokens, not extra LLM calls.
- Scope the prime set to prior findings on blocking criteria that scored near/above the bar; charitable
  ("may be resolved") framing to avoid anchoring. Prior art: the deferred cross-patchset memory (super-path-bag).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pass-1 receives the ticket's prior-review findings as re-verify candidates, deduped by `norm_id`.
- [ ] No additional LLM calls beyond the existing finder pass (context injection only).
- [ ] Test: a finding present in a prior review but dropped on a re-run is re-surfaced; a resolved prior
      finding is dropped by Pass-2, not re-blocked.
- [ ] Recall lift measured on the same-`material_fingerprint` replicate pairs vs the no-memory baseline.
data.title: Plan-review Pass-1 finding-memory (recall fix)
